Good morning, Paula. Welcome to Zurich.
Good morning, David. Thank you.
This is the reception. The entrance is behind you, and the elevator is next to the stairs.
Excuse me, David. Where is the meeting room?
There are three meeting rooms on the second floor. Our meeting is at ten, in room two.
Is there a bathroom on this floor?
Yes, there is. It's next to the elevator.
And is there a coffee area near here?
There is one on the second floor, next to the meeting rooms. There isn't one on this floor.
Perfect. Thank you, David.
Excuse me, can you help me?
Where is the meeting room?
Is there a bathroom near here?
The elevator is next to the entrance.

Hi, Paula. David here. Tomorrow is your first day, so let me tell you about the building. The entrance is on Avenida Paulista. There is a reception on the first floor — say your name there, and they give you your badge. The elevator is next to the stairs. Our team is on the second floor. There are three meeting rooms there, and there is a coffee area next to them. Is there a bathroom on the first floor? Yes, there is — next to the elevator. One more thing, and this is important: there isn't a parking space for visitors. Come by taxi, or by metro. See you at nine. Goodbye.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. In November we travel to Miami — you and me. So, listen. At the airport, everything is a question. Where is the gate? Is there a taxi near the exit? Where is the bathroom? Ask. Don't do what I do — I don't ask, and then I walk in circles for an hour. I am fluent in English, and I still walk in circles. Asking is not about your English. It is about your time. Now, the hotel: there is a hotel next to the office, and there are two restaurants in the hotel. One more thing: at the airport there are two terminals, and our gate is in terminal two. Ask at the entrance, and they help you. See you in November, Paula.
